summary: Smoky Dark Star→Slipknot! jam, tight Brown Eyed Women, and rare Ship Of Fools
blurb: The second set shines with a scorching Dark Star → Slipknot! sequence that anchors the night. Jerry’s vocal on Stella Blue and Phil’s buttery bass on Brown Eyed Women are standout moments. The show closes with an unusual Ship Of Fools encore.
review: July 25, 1974 at Chicago’s International Amphitheatre delivered a classic mid‑’70s Dead experience. The first set rolls smoothly through the usual favorites—Me And My Uncle, Sugaree and a crisp Weather Report Suite that segues into Let It Grow—but it’s the second set that truly elevates this show. A wordless Dark Star stretches into an exhilarating Slipknot! before melting into a heartfelt Stella Blue, showcasing Jerry’s inventive improvisation and soaring vocal on the ballad. Keith Godchaux adds his signature “plinky‑winky” electric piano flair on Row Jimmy and weaves creative textures through the jam. Phil Lesh provides a rich bass tone that grounds Brown Eyed Women and shines on Jack Straw, while Bill Kreutzmann’s jazzy drumming fuels the exploratory moments. The night rounds out with an unexpected Ship Of Fools encore, giving the concert a slightly down‑beat but memorable finish.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across recordings: two reviews label the performance as 'smoking Dark Star>Slipknot!' and 'epic second‑set', while a third calls it 'solid' with praised vocal on Stella Blue. No consistent negative comments appear, so a robust +1.4 is applied.

best_recording.reason: Excellent, crisp soundboard capture with clear instrument separation; highlights the Dark Star→Slipknot! jam and delivers the cleanest overall listening experience.

key_highlights: Smoldering Dark Star → Slipknot! marathon anchors the second set.
Tight Brown Eyed Women featuring Phil’s rich bass tone.
Jerry’s emotive vocal on Stella Blue and sweet slide on Row Jimmy.
Rare Ship Of Fools encore adds a unique finish.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers inventive improvisation in Dark Star, sweet slide on Row Jimmy, and a front‑and‑center vocal performance on Stella Blue; his tone shines especially on Slipknot!.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a buttery bass tone that drives Brown Eyed Women and takes a lead role on Jack Straw; solid foundation throughout the set.
band_performance.Keith: Adds characteristic ‘plinky‑winky’ electric piano textures on Row Jimmy and contributes creative ideas during the Dark Star jam, enhancing the harmonic depth.
band_performance.Billy: Shows jazzy, engaging drumming in the Dark Star segment, helping propel the improvisational flow.
